content.problemStatement:
Identify and correct the logic to check if a number is even. Program should print `true` if number is even, else `false`.


Input Format

An integer N


Output Format

`true` if N is even, else `false`.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output "true" if N is even, otherwise "false". Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: 5 is odd → false. Second test case: 4 is even → true.
0 is even, -7 is odd, -8 is even.
1,000,000,000 is divisible by 2, so the answer is true.

solution.approach: For each integer N we only need to know whether it is divisible by 2. The parity of an integer can be obtained with the modulo operator: N % 2. If the remainder is 0 the number is even, otherwise it is odd. The algorithm iterates over the T test cases, applies this constant‑time check and prints the corresponding string. This is optimal because any solution must read the input at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in O(1) time.
